summary: Fix commit addresses all three W-level findings from berendt's review #1: (1) networkPolicy doc row scope creep removed, (2) Chainsaw E2E test added for resources feature, (3) TODO comments added to Job/CronJob reconcilers for resource propagation. Additionally addresses both I-level findings: default values extracted to exported constants (single source of truth), and empty-ResourceRequirements edge case handled. Code is clean, tests pass, patterns followed.

tests_checklist.item: Resource defaulting tested: nil, empty, explicit preserved
Resource validation tested: valid, CPU exceeds, memory exceeds, both exceed, nil, requests-only
Deployment builder tested: default and custom resources
E2E chainsaw test covers default propagation and custom patch
Edge case: empty ResourceRequirements triggers defaulting (TestDefault_ResourcesSetWhenEmpty)

code_quality_checklist.item: Default values extracted to exported constants (DRY)
DeepCopy() on resource.Quantity vars prevents mutation of package-level defaults
TODO comments reference CC-0042 and point to containerResources() pattern
Follows existing codebase patterns (gomega, chainsaw, envtest)

security_checklist.item: No new input surfaces or secret handling

architecture_checklist.item: Single source of truth for default values via exported vars
E2E test follows chainsaw pattern from sibling tests
Review patterns captured for future reviews

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Literal values replaced with constants across 4 files (webhook, webhook_test, integration_test, deployment_test)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Empty-but-non-nil ResourceRequirements caught by defaulting webhook
Validation accumulates all errors without short-circuiting

defensive_checklist.item: DeepCopy() prevents mutation of package-level Quantity vars
Nil-safe handling in containerResources() and defaulting webhook

issues_found.description: Documentation defaulting table says condition is `== nil` but the code now also handles non-nil empty ResourceRequirements (`resources: {}`). The condition column should reflect the actual implementation behavior.
issues_found.fix: Update the Condition column from `== nil` to `== nil or empty` to match the webhook code at keystone_webhook.go:80 which checks `obj.Spec.Resources == nil || (len(obj.Spec.Resources.Requests) == 0 && len(obj.Spec.Resources.Limits) == 0)`.
issues_found.evidence: | `spec.resources` | `== nil` | `{requests: ...}` |  — but code does: if obj.Spec.Resources == nil || (len(obj.Spec.Resources.Requests) == 0 && len(obj.Spec.Resources.Limits) == 0)

suggested_improvements: Consider adding a test for partially-set resources (e.g., only Requests set, Limits empty) to verify the defaulting webhook does not overwrite partial user configuration — currently this behavior is correct but untested.

detected_patterns.name: Exported default constants with DeepCopy for webhook defaulting
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding a new defaulting webhook that injects Kubernetes resource types (e.g., ResourceRequirements, resource.Quantity) as defaults — export the default values as package-level vars and use DeepCopy() when assigning them to prevent mutation of the shared variables
detected_patterns.description: Default values for webhook-injected Kubernetes types are declared as exported package-level vars (e.g., DefaultMemoryRequest, DefaultCPULimit) using resource.MustParse(). The defaulting webhook uses DeepCopy() when assigning these to the spec to prevent mutation of the package-level variables. Tests reference the exported constants directly rather than duplicating literal values, establishing a single source of truth. This pattern is currently used in one operator (Keystone) but is an architectural decision that will recur as new operators add resource defaulting.
